blocks.body: Backshift of Tenses explains how tenses often move back after a past reporting verb.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"Backshift means that after a reporting verb in the past, the tense in the reported clause often moves one step back.","rules":[{"label":"Typical tense shift","color":"emerald","text":"The most common changes are present → past, past → past perfect, and will → would.","example":"I am tired. → He said that he was tired."},{"label":"Meaning comes first","color":"blue","text":"Backshift is not a mechanical rule. Check whether the situation is still true or whether the sentence expresses a general fact.","example":"The guide said that the museum opens at nine."},{"label":"Time references still matter","color":"amber","text":"Besides the tense, words of time, pronouns, and the situation itself may also change.","example":"We will finish tomorrow. → They said that they would finish the next day."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. The most common changes
blocks.body:
Direct formReported formExample
Present SimplePast Simple“I live in Kyiv.” → She said that she lived in Kyiv.
Present ContinuousPast Continuous“I am working.” → He said that he was working.
Past SimplePast Perfect“We finished.” → They said that they had finished.
Present PerfectPast Perfect“I have seen it.” → She said that she had seen it.
willwould“I will call you.” → He said that he would call me.
cancould“I can help.” → She said that she could help.

blocks.heading: 2. When backshift is typical and when it is not
blocks.body:
• Backshift is most common when the reporting verb is in the past: said, told, asked, explained.
  • Backshift can be optional if the fact is still true: She said that Paris is the capital of France.
  • Writers and speakers often avoid backshift when they want to stress that the information is still true now.
  • If the reporting verb is in the present, backshift is usually unnecessary: He says that he is busy.

blocks.heading: 3. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: keeping will after a past reporting verb.
    Better: She said that she would come later.
  • Mistake: forcing backshift even in stable facts.
    Possible: The teacher said that water boils at 100°C.
  • Mistake: focusing only on the tense and forgetting tomorrow / now / here. In reported speech, more than the tense may need to change.

blocks.heading: 4. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• After a past reporting verb, backshift is very common, but it is not always obligatory.
  • The most frequent changes are present → past, past → past perfect, will → would, and can → could.
  • Always check whether the fact is still true and whether you want to emphasise that truth now.
